title: 10 best herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for urinary calculus are a blend of natural oils extracted from plants, which help in dissolving and flushing out kidney stones. These oils are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and promote the removal of stones from the urinary tract. The best herbal essential oils for urinary calculus include Juniperus communis, Berberis vulgaris, Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, and others. If left untreated, urinary calculus can lead to severe pain, nausea, and even kidney damage, causing immense suffering and impacting daily life, hindering work, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper essential oils helps with urinary calculus because it has natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to flush out small stones from the kidneys and bladder. The oil also relieves spasms in the urinary tract, reducing pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones. Additionally, monk's pepper essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent bacterial infections that can worsen urinary calculus symptoms. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to alleviate symptoms and promote overall urinary health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Vitex agnus-castus has been traditionally used to relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, which could help alleviate symptoms associated with urinary tract stones.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the urinary tract, potentially reducing pain and discomfort caused by kidney stones.

Antioxidant properties: Vitex agnus-castus contains antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the kidneys and urinary tract, which may contribute to the development of kidney stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the monk's pepper plant are used due to their ability to stimulate the kidneys and bladder, helping to relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections and kidney stones.
Barks: The barks of the monk's pepper plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the urinary tract, making them effective for managing symptoms of urinary calculus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cnidium monnieri leaves and stems in abundance for a total of 100g.
Dry the plant material using a shade or low-temperature dryer for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material with 20% distilled water for a total of 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil through a condenser and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight or heat for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as curcuminoids and volatile oils, work together to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, making it easier for the body to pass kidney stones or other types of calculi. Additionally, turmeric essential oil's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help combat infections that can exacerbate calculus formation, promoting a healthy and balanced urinary system.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Property: Turmeric Essential Oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and discomfort in the urinary tract, making it easier for stones to pass through.

Antioxidant Property: The antioxidant properties of Turmeric Essential Oil help protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of further stone formation and promoting healing.
Urolithic Property: Turmeric Essential Oil's urolithic property helps dissolve and break down existing kidney stones, allowing for easier passage through the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus.
Roots: The roots of turmeric contain volatile oils and saponins that help to dissolve and remove calculi from the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis aristata leaves and stems, drying them for at least two weeks in a warm dry place.
Combine 100 grams of dried plant material with 500 milliliters of carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the infused oil to settle for at least 24 hours before transferring it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compound, curcumin, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the kidneys and bladder, making it an effective natural remedy for treating symptoms associated with kidney stones. Additionally, turmeric essential oils have been found to increase urine flow and help dissolve existing stones, reducing the risk of future stone formation. This natural treatment offers a safe and non-invasive alternative for managing urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric essential oil helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, which can alleviate discomfort and pain caused by urinary calculi (kidney stones).

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Turmeric essential oil's antibacterial and antifungal properties help prevent bacterial infections that can contribute to the formation of urinary calculi and promote healing of the urinary tract.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Turmeric essential oil contains compounds like curcumin, which have been shown to reduce pain and discomfort associated with urinary calculi by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make essential oils due to their high content of bioactive compounds, such as curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus.
Roots: Turmeric roots are a rich source of medicinal properties, including volatile oils and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and ease pain related to urinary stones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots extract sufficient material for processing weighing about 100 grams.
Dry the extracted roots in a warm air circulation oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind dried turmeric roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle then sift through a 100-mesh sieve to obtain uniform particles.
Steam distill 500 grams of powdered turmeric roots with 1 liter of distilled water for 2-3 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it using activated charcoal and cotton filters before bottling weighing approximately 10-15%.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pomegranate essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's compounds, such as ellagic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to kidney stone formation. Additionally, pomegranate oil's flavonoids may help reduce inflammation in the kidneys and urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus. Furthermore, its diuretic properties can increase urine production, potentially preventing the formation of new stones or aiding in their passage.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and pain associated with urinary tract infections (UTIs) and kidney stone passage, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms.
Dissolving agent: Pomegranate essential oil's ability to dissolve calcium oxalate crystals, a common component of kidney stones, helps to break down and pass them through the urine more easily.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and polyphenols that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, making them effective against kidney stone formation.
Barks: Contains alkaloids and tannins that have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, including kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pomegranate flowers and leaves, collecting a minimum of 100 grams for processing.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a drying machine for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using 10 kilograms of water for 4 hours at a temperature of 100°c.
Collect and filter the distilled essential oil, retaining only 50 milliliters of the resulting liquid.
Store the punica granatum essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yew essential oils helps with urinary calculus because it has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms associated with kidney stones, including pain and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of yew oil help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate some of the pain and discomfort caused by kidney stones. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of taxus baccata, ensuring a minimum quantity of 500 grams, for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours, until crispy and brittle.
Steam-distill the dried leaves in a stainless steel still with 1 liter of distilled water for 4 hours, maintaining a temperature of 100°c to 105°c.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding any sediment or impurities.
Store the final taxus baccata essential oil in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months, ensuring optimal potency and stability by protecting from light and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with kidney stones. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with urinary calculi.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful for treating urinary tract infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots weigh approximately 1000 grams.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area for 14 days at room temperature.
Grind dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours and filter through cheesecloth to obtain the essential oil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh solidago virgaurea flowers weighing approximately 250 grams and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until they reach a moisture content of 5%.
Grind the dried flowers into a coarse powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Steam distill the ground flowers using a clevenger apparatus with water at a ratio of 1:2 for 4 hours and collect the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and store it in dark glass bottles.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat urinary calculus with herbal essential oils is by combining certain blends that promote urine flow and help dissolve kidney stones. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to urinary calculus that are treated with herbal essential oils are gallstones, kidney stones, and bladder stones. Certain herbs like juniper berry, uva ursi, and stone root have been traditionally used to treat these conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus communis essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, stimulate the kidneys to increase urine production, helping to flush out small stones and gravel. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making it easier for the body to pass stones. This combination of properties makes Juniperus communis essential oils a popular natural remedy for urinary calculus. Regular use may help prevent further stone formation.
remedy_constituents: Terpinen-4-ol: This monoterpene acts as a diuretic, increasing urine production to help flush out kidney stones and reduce their formation.
α-Pinene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with urinary calculus.
Bornyl acetate: This sesquiterpene has antispasmodic properties, which help relieve muscle spasms and cramps in the urinary tract caused by kidney stones.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils due to their diuretic properties, helping to increase urine production and relieve symptoms associated with urinary calculus.
Barks: They are used for their antiseptic and diuretic properties, which aid in preventing infections and promoting urination to help dissolve kidney stones.
Fruits: They are used due to their diuretic and antiseptic properties, which help to increase urine production and prevent infections that can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh juniperus communis needles and berries by hand in the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested plant material with 2 liters of water for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Collect the obtained distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for 24 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron membrane filter to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and infection in the urinary tract. remedy_constituents: Berberine: A quaternary ammonium alkaloid that helps to reduce the formation of kidney stones by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to the development of urinary tract infections, which can lead to the formation of stones.

Oleanolic acid: A triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to the formation of kidney stones, as well as help to break down and dissolve existing stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of berberine, a compound known for its diuretic and antiseptic properties.

Barks: Berberine present in the barks of Berberis vulgaris helps in its use as a remedy for urinary calculus, among other conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the berberis vulgaris roots and stems in the early morning after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100-150 degrees celsius for 2 hours using a 5kg sample and 10 liters of distilled water.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separator and transfer it to a clean glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of arctostaphylos uva-ursi leaves in the summer months for optimal oil production.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 80-90 degrees fahrenheit for 8-12 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours to extract essential oils.
Separate the extracted essential oil from the distillate using a separator or decanter within 24 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter to remove any impurities and improve quality.

Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus oxycedrus essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its diuretic properties, which increase urine production and help flush out the kidneys and urinary tract. The oil's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of kidney stones. Additionally, Juniperus oxycedrus essential oils have been traditionally used to dissolve and break down existing stones, making it easier to pass them out of the body, promoting a healthy and functioning urinary system. This natural remedy can provide relief from painful kidney stones.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: This monoterpene helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the urinary tract, facilitating the passage of urinary calculi.
Sabinene: As a monoterpene, sabinene has diuretic properties that help increase urine production and flow, which aids in flushing out urinary calculi from the system.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with urinary calculus, while also promoting the expulsion of kidney stones.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds of Juniperus communis are used to make essential oils for urinary calculus because of their diuretic properties that help in flushing out kidney stones.
Leaves: The leaves of Juniperus communis are used to make essential oils for urinary calculus because of their ability to stimulate urine production and help dissolve kidney stones.

Fruits: The fruits of Juniperus communis are used to make essential oils for urinary calculus because of their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties that help alleviate pain and promote urine flow.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to increase urine production, which can help to flush out small stones and prevent their formation. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce pain and discomfort associated with urinary calculi. The oil's potential to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract may also help to prevent further stone formation, providing relief for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin acts as a diuretic, increasing urine production to help flush out small stones and prevent their formation.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass small stones.
Saponins: Saponins have mild antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent infections and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass small stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Arctium lappa are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of inulin, a polysaccharide that can help dissolve kidney stones.
Seeds: The seeds of Arctium lappa are used to make essential oils, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus.
Leaves: The leaves of Arctium lappa are used to make essential oils, which may contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with urinary tract issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctium lappa roots and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the arctium lappa roots and leaves by spreading them out in a single layer.
Grind the dried arctium lappa roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the arctium lappa powder in a still at 100 to 150 degrees celsius.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillation process using a coffee filter.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with urinary calculus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as anethole and limonene, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with kidney stones. Additionally, the oil's diuretic properties encourage the passage of urine, facilitating the removal of stones from the urinary tract. By promoting a healthy balance of fluids and reducing stone-forming substances, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils support the natural process of eliminating urinary calculi.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: This terpene helps with urinary calculus by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract and preventing the formation of kidney stones.
Daucosterol: This triterpenoid acts as a diuretic, increasing urine production and helping to flush out minerals that can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.
Foeniculin: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the severity of symptoms associated with urinary calculus, such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used due to their anethole content, which has been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of urinary stones.
Leaves: Leaves of Fennel contain a compound called estragole, which is known for its carminative properties and may help in reducing the formation of kidney stones.
Seeds (second item): Fennel seeds have also been traditionally used to help stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence, which may be beneficial for people with urinary calculus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare stems and flowers in the morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature range of 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled water and essential oil mixture and separate the oil from the water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities or contaminants from the oil.
Store the extracted foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool dark place in glass bottles at a ratio of 1-2% of the oil to a carrier oil.
